title: Restore Data From Paused Free Cluster

sections.heading: Prerequisites
sections.body:
Before you begin, you need the following:

- [Project Owner](<Project Owner>) role in Atlas

- mongodump

- mongorestore

sections.heading: Step 1: Deploy an  or higher cluster.
sections.body:
You must deploy an `M10` cluster with the same MongoDB
[version](supported-mdb-versions) as the paused Free cluster.
To learn more about deploying an `M10` or higher
cluster with a specific MongoDB version, see
[Create a Cluster](create-new-cluster). If you use the Atlas UI to create
the cluster, click **Advanced Settings** to
[Select the MongoDB Version of the Cluster](create-cluster-version). If your paused Free cluster
was running a MongoDB version that has since reached EOL
(End of Life), you won't be able to deploy an `M10` cluster
with the same version as the Free cluster.

Wait until your cluster is deployed before proceeding to the
next step.

> **Important:** You must delete the `M10` cluster at the end of this
> procedure to avoid charges.

sections.heading: Step 2: Access the available backups for the paused Free cluster.
sections.body:
You can access the available backups for a cluster at the
following URL (Uniform Resource Locator):

```shell
https://cloud.mongodb.com/v2/<project_id>#/clusters/backup/<cluster_name>
```

Where:

- `project_id` is the unique 24-hexadecimal digit string that
  identifies your project.

- `cluster_name` is the label that identifies your cluster.

sections.heading: Step 3: Restore data from the backup.
sections.body:
- For the snapshot that you want to restore data from, click
  **Restore** in the **Actions** column.

- In the **Restore** window, select the project that contains
  the `M10` cluster from the **Target Project** dropdown
  if it isn't already selected.

- In the **Restore** window, select the `M10` cluster
  from the **Target Cluster** dropdown if it isn't already
  selected.

- Click **Restore**.

sections.heading: Step 4: Upgrade your  cluster.
sections.body:
You must upgrade your `M10` cluster to the latest supported MongoDB
version. To learn more, see [upgrade your cluster to MongoDB 8.0](upgrade-major-MongoDB-version). Wait for
the upgrade to complete before proceeding to the next step.

sections.heading: Step 5: Export the data on the  cluster.
sections.body:
You can download a copy of the data on your `M10` cluster by
using mongodump. To learn more, see [mongodump reference page](database-tools/mongodump).

sections.heading: Step 6: Deploy a Free cluster.
sections.body: To learn more, see [Deploy a Free Cluster](deploy-atlas-m0-cluster).

sections.heading: Step 7: Upload data to your Free cluster.
sections.body:
You must upload the data that you downloaded from the `M10`
cluster to your Free cluster. You can use the
mongorestore utility to restore the data into your Free cluster.
To learn more, see [mongorestore reference page](database-tools/mongorestore).

sections.heading: Step 8: Delete the  cluster.
sections.body:
To learn more, see [Terminate a Cloud Deployment](terminate-atlas-cluster). You must delete
your `M10` cluster to avoid charges.
